articles.headline: Wisconsin center Renfro in question for opener with injury
articles.description: Wisconsin center Jake Renfro suffered what school officials described as a lower-body injury in practice, putting his status for the start of the season in question.

articles.headline: 'Healthy, vibrant' Sanders expects Colorado to 'win differently'
articles.description: After a recent cancer scare, Colorado coach Deion Sanders says he's feeling "healthy and vibrant" ahead of his team's season opener against Georgia Tech on Aug. 29 in Boulder.

images.name: Deion Sanders announces bladder cancer battle
images.caption: Colorado coach Deion Sanders and his medical crew reveal that he battled and beat bladder cancer.

articles.headline: Betting buzz: Manning no longer favorite to go No. 1 in 2026 NFL draft
articles.description: Arch Manning's odds to be the No. 1 pick in the 2026 NFL draft drastically shifted after Archie Manning said his grandson would stay at Texas.

images.name: Arch Manning [1296x729]
images.caption: Arch Manning now has the fifth-shortest odds to be the No. 1 pick in the 2026 NFL draft after his grandfather's comments on Thursday.

images.name: Jacob Misiorowski [1296x729]
images.caption: Jacob Misiorowski's odds to win NL Rookie of the Year have fallen from -220 to +900.

images.name: Dak Prescott of Dallas Cowboys [608x342]
images.caption: The Cowboys won seven games last year, and sportsbooks aren't too keen on them doing much better this season.

images.name: Should Arch Manning stay at Texas beyond this season?
images.caption: The "Get Up" crew breaks down Arch Manning's path to the NFL and debates whether he should stay at Texas after this season.

articles.headline: Boise State, Colorado State, Utah State sue Mountain West
articles.description: Boise State, Colorado State and Utah State filed an updated lawsuit arguing the Mountain West and commissioner Gloria Nevarez willfully disregarded the league's bylaws by "intentionally and fraudulently" depriving the schools of their membership rights.

articles.headline: Where is 'College GameDay'? 2025 schedule, locations, recaps
articles.description: It's time for a new season of "College GameDay" on ESPN! Check out the event hub page for weekly site details, recaps and other information.

articles.headline: LSU QB Garrett Nussmeier dealing with patellar tendinitis
articles.description: LSU quarterback Garrett Nussmeier is dealing with patellar tendinitis in his knee after aggravating the issue in practice this week, according to coach Brian Kelly.

images.name: Garrett Nussmeier of the LSU Tigers warms up before a game against the Baylor Bears in the Kinder's Texas Bowl at NRG Stadium on December 31, 2024 in Houston, Texas. [600x400]
images.alt: Garrett Nussmeier of the LSU Tigers warms up before a game against the Baylor Bears in the Kinder's Texas Bowl at NRG Stadium on December 31, 2024 in Houston, Texas.

images.name: Kelly: QB Nussmeier's run game will be crucial to LSU's success
images.caption: The SEC Now crew delves into Garrett Nussmeier's potential on the run and the Tigers' new approach, specifically to their high-stakes opener against Clemson.

article.headline: Montee Ball moves into 2nd on career TD list in rout
article.description: Montee Ball ran for 198 yards and three touchdowns, James White scored on runs of 69 and 50 yards, and Wisconsin set a school rushing record Saturday with 564 yards in a 62-14 rout at Indiana.
article.linkText: Badgers' Ball moves into 2nd on TD list in win

inlines.body:
More From ESPN.com

It's not the road Wisconsin expected to travel back to the Big Ten title game, but it's back to Indianapolis for the Badgers, writes Adam Rittenburg. Blog

Move Over, Ricky

Montee Ball scored three times against Indiana on Saturday to move past Ricky Williams and into sole possession of second on the all-time FBS touchdown list. Here's a look:

Years Players TDs
1996-99 Travis Prentice, M-OH 78
2009-12 Montee Ball, WIS 77*
1995-98 Ricky Williams, TEX 75
2002-05 Tau. Henderson, TTU 69
1999-2002 Brock Forsey, BSU 68
* 3 TDs on Saturday vs. Indiana
-- ESPN Stats & Information

related.headline: Montee Ball passes Ricky Williams
related.title: Montee Ball of Wisconsin Badgers moves into 2nd on career TD list
related.linkText: Badgers' Ball moves into 2nd on career TD list

article.story: BLOOMINGTON, Ind. -- Montee Ball and James White put Wisconsin's Big Ten title hopes back on track Saturday. The two running backs ran for 359 yards and five touchdowns and Ball moved into second place on the career touchdown list, leading the Badgers to a 62-14 rout at Indiana and back to the Big Ten title game. Wisconsin finished with a school-record 564 yards rushing and crushed the Memorial Stadium record for an opponent -- 441 yards by Michigan State in 1974 and matched by Northwestern in 1999. Ball's big day probably should have been expected. He got his first career start against Indiana two years ago, and in his three previous games against the Hoosiers averaged 141.3 yards on the ground. On Saturday, with Wisconsin (7-3, 4-2) coming off a bye week after a rare home loss to Michigan State, Ball was better than usual. He carried 27 times for 198 yards and scored three times, passing 1998 Heisman Trophy winner Ricky Williams for second on the FBS list with 77 career scores. Travis Prentice of Miami (Ohio) is the all-time leader with 78. Williams had 75. White had 14 carries for 161 yards and scored on a 69-yard run with 13 seconds left in the first half and a 50-yard run in the fourth quarter. But the big reward for Wisconsin was clinching a spot in next month's Big Ten title game and getting a chance to defend its title. Wisconsin has won the last two Big Ten titles, and with Ohio State and Penn State ineligible for postseason play now needs only one more victory to claim a third straight conference crown. For Indiana (4-6, 2-4), it was a huge letdown. A victory not only would have given the Hoosiers their first three-game winning streak in league play since 1993, it also would have put Indiana in position to control its own destiny in the title chase. Instead, the Hoosiers' school-record streak of 10 straight games with 24 or more points ended, too. Indiana never had a chance against Wisconsin's rushers. It took the Badgers one play -- a 19-yard end-around -- to match its entire rushing total against Michigan State. Six plays later, on third-and-2, Ball found a huge hole on the left side of the offensive line and ran for 21 yards. He took advantage of a good seal block on the next play, jogging in from 12 yards out to tie Williams for second place. That was only the start. Wisconsin, which only attempted seven passes, ran the ball six straight times on their next possession, then faked out the Hoosiers on third-and-goal from the Indiana 2 when Curt Phillips threw a strike to Sam Arneson for a 14-0 lead. After settling for a 24-yard field goal, Indiana looked like it might get back into the game with a 6-yard TD pass from Cameron Coffman to Ted Bolser with 2:25 left in the half. But White answered as he inexplicably turned a broken play into a 69-yard TD run with 13 seconds left in the first half to make it 24-7. Indiana never recovered. Ball opened the second half with a 1-yard TD plunge, then scored on a 49-yard run and White scored again on the 50-yarder to make it 48-14.
